# Feature: session_start

> Requires: security_no_dangerous_patterns
> Scope: scripts/session-start.sh, hooks/hooks.json
> Stack: shell/bash

## What it does

Session initialization hook that clears stale runtime state when a new Claude Code session begins. Removes the invariant write lock to prevent leftover bypass locks from prior sessions from weakening gate protection. Triggered on startup, clear, and compact events.

## Rules

- RULE-1: The hook triggers on SessionStart events matching `startup|clear|compact`
- RULE-2: Removes `.purlin/runtime/invariant_write_lock` if it exists
- RULE-3: Does not fail if the runtime directory does not exist (no-op)
- RULE-4: Does not fail if the lock file does not exist (no-op)
- RULE-5: Always exits with code 0

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): Read `hooks/hooks.json`; verify SessionStart matcher is `startup|clear|compact` and command points to `session-start.sh`
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): Create `.purlin/runtime/invariant_write_lock` with content `{"target": "i_test.md"}`; run session-start.sh; verify the lock file is deleted @slow
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): Run session-start.sh with `PROJECT_ROOT` pointing to a directory with no `.purlin/runtime/`; verify exit code is 0 @slow
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): Run session-start.sh with `.purlin/runtime/` existing but no lock file; verify exit code is 0 @slow
- PROOF-5 (RULE-5): Run session-start.sh under any condition; verify exit code is 0 @slow
